request.lastMessagePreview: <context> ## Current State PCs: Aldric [length] Terminal: 80 cols × 16 visible rows. [stats] Turn: Aldric </context> [Al

result.text:
You crouch and run your fingers along the oak, feeling the grain. The <color=#20b2aa>iron bands</color> are ancient, rust-flecked but solid—driven deep into the wood with nails the size of your thumb. No weakness here. The lock itself is a heavy mortise, the keyhole clogged with a century of grime.

As you work, the tavern's ambient noise—the hiss of the hearth, the rain's drumming rhythm—drops away. You realize the <color=#cc8844>hooded figure</color> has gone silent. When you glance up, they're watching you. Not obviously—a slight tilt of the head, one eye visible beneath the cowl. The gaze holds for a breath, then slides away.

<color=#44cc44>Mella</color> sets down her mug with deliberate force. "That door stays locked," she says flatly. "Has since before you were born, most like. Nothing down there worth bothering."

The lie is obvious. Her jaw's tight. She's waiting to see if you'll push.

What do you do?
